Thanks @StormLiangMS — addressed the post-`configure_vxlan_switch` concern by adding a `wait_until` check for the SWITCH_TABLE entry in APP_DB.

Regarding the `config_reload` point: there's actually no `config_reload` in `create_vxlan_vnet_config()` — config is applied via `sonic-cfggen --write-to-db`, and VXLAN tunnel presence is already verified with `wait_until(60, 5, 5, _check_vxlan_tunnel_config)` before calling `configure_vxlan_switch`. The `config_reload` in the file is only in the cleanup path (`restore_config`).
Azure Pipelines failed to run 1 pipeline(s).

Hi @StormLiangMS — thanks for the second look! Pushed a fix to assert the `wait_until` after `configure_vxlan_switch`.

To clarify the config_reload concern: there's actually no `config_reload` in `create_vxlan_vnet_config()`. The config is applied via `sonic-cfggen --write-to-db`, and the VXLAN tunnel presence is verified with `wait_until(60, 5, 5, _check_vxlan_tunnel_config)` before calling `configure_vxlan_switch`. The `config_reload` you may be seeing in the file is only in the cleanup/restore path.

Changes in this push:
- `wait_until` after `configure_vxlan_switch` is now wrapped with `pytest_assert` to catch failures explicitly

Ready for re-review.

Fixed the build, and tested this locally it seems to be working.

@nate-nexthop , I cherry-picked your changes and ran the complete container hardening module. -------------------- **Observation 1:-** Previously, the test cases were parameterized per Docker container, with one test case per container. Because of this, the total test case count was around 20. With the recent changes, this validation is now being handled within a single test case instead of separate parameterized ones. As a result, the overall test case count has been reduced. -------------------- **Observation 2:-** For the newly added test case `test_container_block_device_mounted`, I observe that it is being skipped in my scenario with the following message: Skipped: got empty parameter set for (enum_rand_one_per_hwsku_hostname, enum_rand_one_asic_index, enum_dut_feature) However, with the previous version of the code, this test case was executing successfully and was not being skipped. -----------------

> @nate-nexthop , I cherry-picked your changes and ran the complete container hardening module. > > **Observation 1:-** Previously, the test cases were parameterized per Docker container, with one test case per container. Because of this, the total test case count was around 20. > > With the recent changes, this validation is now being handled within a single test case instead of separate parameterized ones. As a result, the overall test case count has been reduced. > > **Observation 2:-** For the newly added test case `test_container_block_device_mounted`, I observe that it is being skipped in my scenario with the following message: > > Skipped: got empty parameter set for (enum_rand_one_per_hwsku_hostname, enum_rand_one_asic_index, enum_dut_feature) > > However, with the previous version of the code, this test case was executing successfully and was not being skipped. > > The original test case `test_container_privileged` was renamed to `test_container_block_device_mounted` to more accurately describe what it is testing. The conditions for running/skipping `test_container_block_device_mounted` are the same as they were before, so the number of test cases is the same. For the new test, `test_container_privileged` just runs on all the dockers in one check. I don't know why you're seeing `test_container_block_device_mounted` skipped, checking one of the automated test runs on this PR shows it running: https://elastictest.org/scheduler/publictestplan/699727f5f7886b9b342afef3?testcase=container_hardening%2Ftest_container_hardening.py&type=console

> > @nate-nexthop , I cherry-picked your changes and ran the complete container hardening module. > > **Observation 1:-** Previously, the test cases were parameterized per Docker container, with one test case per container. Because of this, the total test case count was around 20. > > With the recent changes, this validation is now being handled within a single test case instead of separate parameterized ones. As a result, the overall test case count has been reduced. > > **Observation 2:-** For the newly added test case `test_container_block_device_mounted`, I observe that it is being skipped in my scenario with the following message: > > Skipped: got empty parameter set for (enum_rand_one_per_hwsku_hostname, enum_rand_one_asic_index, enum_dut_feature) > > However, with the previous version of the code, this test case was executing successfully and was not being skipped. > > > > The original test case `test_container_privileged` was renamed to `test_container_block_device_mounted` to more accurately describe what it is testing. The conditions for running/skipping `test_container_block_device_mounted` are the same as they were before, so the number of test cases is the same. For the new test, `test_container_privileged` just runs on all the dockers in one check. > > I don't know why you're seeing `test_container_block_device_mounted` skipped, checking one of the automated test runs on this PR shows it running: > > https://elastictest.org/scheduler/publictestplan/699727f5f7886b9b342afef3?testcase=container_hardening%2Ftest_container_hardening.py&type=console Hi @nate-nexthop, Thanks for your response. It looks like the issue was with my local cloned repository and/or the DUT not being in a good state. I have now freshly configured everything with a newly cloned repo, and I can confirm that it is working as expected. -----------------

> It looks like the issue was with my local cloned repository and/or the DUT not being in a good state. I have now freshly configured everything with a newly cloned repo, and I can confirm that it is working as expected.
> 
> 

Glad to hear it's working for you now!

Notes:

1) compare_flow_tables_pdsctl() returns None when ASIC isn’t Pensando. If callers treat it as bool, this may silently pass. Consider skipping explicitly or returning False consistently.

2) parse_pdsctl_show_flow_output() assumes each data line has exactly 10 columns. If pdsctl output changes (extra columns / tokens), zip() will silently truncate. Consider validating column count and logging/skip when mismatch.

> 2. parse_pdsctl_show_flow_output() assumes each data line has exactly 10 columns. If pdsctl output changes (extra columns / tokens), zip() will silently truncate. Consider validating column count and logging/skip when mismatch.

HI @venu-nexthop, I'm not sure this change is correct. According to test plan https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/blob/master/docs/testplan/Everflow-test-plan.md#test-objective-5, the egress port should remain to be the old one if new next hop is added.

> HI @venu-nexthop, I'm not sure this change is correct. According to test plan https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/blob/master/docs/testplan/Everflow-test-plan.md#test-objective-5, the egress port should remain to be the old one if new next hop is added.

Thanks for pointing the testplan @bingwang-ms . Let me check mirroring code a bit to understand what makes this assumption to hold (some recent changes might have broken that causing these failures). I was going by the normal behavior of ECMP that adding another nexthop may hash packets to the new one.
/azp run
Azure Pipelines successfully started running 1 pipeline(s).

> > HI @venu-nexthop, I'm not sure this change is correct. According to test plan https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/blob/master/docs/testplan/Everflow-test-plan.md#test-objective-5, the egress port should remain to be the old one if new next hop is added.
> 
> Thanks for pointing the testplan @bingwang-ms . Let me check mirroring code a bit to understand what makes this assumption to hold (some recent changes might have broken that causing these failures). I was going by the normal behavior of ECMP that adding another nexthop may hash packets to the new one.

@bingwang-ms I looked at the mirroring code. For non-VOQ switches, mirror destination is selected explicitly from one of the ECMP nexthops but for VOQ systems the destination is recycle/recirc port. After the packet gets recycled, it is forwarded as a normal L3 packet and can take any ECMP nexthop. So this test test_everflow_remove_unused_ecmp_next_hop need to be skipped similar to test_everflow_remove_used_ecmp_next_hop for VOQ DUTS. Updated diff accordingly.
@abdosi Can you help review this change?

> @abdosi Can you help review this change?

@abdosi Appreciate any feedback on this.
An open question is how this test passed with other DNX platforms that also use recycle port as the mirror destination.

> An open question is how this test passed with other DNX platforms that also use recycle port as the mirror destination.

Yes, need to confirm if it is passing for other DNX systems. Another similar test is already being skipped for VOQ systems: https://github.com/nexthop-ai/private-sonic-mgmt/blob/master/tests/everflow/test_everflow_testbed.py#L602.

Two quick notes:

1) _check_route_propagation uses ROUTE_TABLE:<prefix> without mask. For IPv6, Redis keys are typically prefix/mask (e.g., 2001:db8:100::1/128). Suggest using f"{prefix}/{mask}" for the key.
2) The module fixture now depends on ip_version — just confirming this fixture is defined/available for this test, otherwise it may error.

> Two quick notes:
> 
> 1. _check_route_propagation uses ROUTE_TABLE: without mask. For IPv6, Redis keys are typically prefix/mask (e.g., 2001:db8:100::1/128). Suggest using f"{prefix}/{mask}" for the key.
> 2. The module fixture now depends on ip_version — just confirming this fixture is defined/available for this test, otherwise it may error.
